title: bupleurum stellatum f. vulgare
intro: Bupleurum stellatum f. vulgare, commonly known as Hare's ear or Bupleurum, is a flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects, and is traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Bupleurum include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Bupleurum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its ability to balance the body's energy and promote overall well-being.
